steps.title: Use circle standard form
steps.content: A circle written in standard form shows both the center and radius directly. In this form, $h$ and $k$ give the center coordinates, and the value on the right is the square of the radius.

steps.title: Match the given equation
steps.content:
Compare the given equation to the standard form.
$$(x-15)^2+(y-k)^2=81$$
The center is $(15,k)$. Since $81=9^2$, the radius is $9$. The answer is A.
